description: The hand/grip orientation or style that distinguishes a movement from its sibling variations (variation facet, §6.3). Open token; unknown values round-trip (§3.3, §5.9). Seeded from common strength-training grip variants; 'pronated' is already in use in data/exercises.json (pull-up).

tokens.label: Pronated Grip
tokens.description: Palms facing away from the body (overhand).

tokens.label: Mixed Grip
tokens.description: One hand pronated, one supinated (common on deadlifts).

tokens.label: Hook Grip
tokens.description: Thumb wrapped inside the fingers against the bar (Olympic lifting).

tokens.label: False Grip
tokens.description: Thumb over the bar rather than wrapped around it (gymnastics rings/muscle-up).

tokens.label: Wide Grip
tokens.description: Hands placed wider than shoulder width.

tokens.label: Close Grip
tokens.description: Hands placed narrower than shoulder width.

tokens.label: Half Crimp
tokens.description: Climbing grip: fingers bent ~90° at the second knuckle, thumb off (standard hangboard testing grip).

tokens.label: Full Crimp
tokens.description: Climbing grip: hyperextended fingertips with the thumb locked over the index finger.

tokens.label: Open Hand
tokens.description: Climbing grip: fingers open/extended over the hold (three-finger drag, sloper grip).
